purpose: Fix the market_resolved force-close path so it persists reason_close and ts_close to the DB. Currently those fields write NULL, breaking the close audit trail. Every closed trade must have a non-NULL reason_close and ts_close so it can be audited, reported, and accounted for correctly.

context.finding: RISK_PIPELINE_AUDIT_REPORT_001.md — broken invariant at bot_core.py:570-577 + core/db.py:131-139
context.current_behavior: close_trade(trade.id, {'exit_px': exit_px, 'pnl_usd': round(pnl, 4), 'fees_usd': 0.0}) — reason_close and ts_close are absent from the dict, so core/db.py writes NULL via .get()
context.required_behavior: close_trade dict must include reason_close='market_resolved' and ts_close=int(time.time())

implementation.where: The market_resolved force-close block in the exit loop — the close_trade() call inside the 'if res and res.get(resolved)' block. Audit report places this at bot_core.py:570-577.
notes: time is already imported in bot_core.py — use time.time(), not datetime.
Confirm the close_trade() signature in core/db.py accepts reason_close and ts_close in the dict before submitting — read core/db.py:130-139 first.
Do not change the CLOSE logger.info line — it already logs the correct information.
Do not change any other close path — only the market_resolved dict.

steps: git diff --name-only  # must show only bot_core.py
grep -n 'reason_close.*market_resolved' bot_core.py  # confirm field is present
grep -n 'ts_close.*time' bot_core.py  # confirm ts_close is present
Manual DB check: write a fake resolved_markets.json for a non-existent market_id, confirm bot loop runs without error

do_not: Touch core/db.py, core/risk.py, or any file other than bot_core.py
Change the standard close path (should_close block) — only the market_resolved block
Change any TP/SL threshold or cooldown value
Add any new fields to the DB schema
